label: userinfo credentials are stripped out of the authority
an api_key value is masked while the key itself stays readable
an apikey value written without a separator is masked
a bearer TOKEN is masked and the Bearer keyword survives
a quoted bearer token inside a headers object is masked the same way
a non-bearer authorization value is masked outright
a sig value is masked and the following query parameter survives
an X-Sas-Token header value is masked
a quoted password value is masked inside its quotes
an access_token value is masked
every secret on a line is masked, not just the first
leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed
NEGATIVE: an ordinary source line passes through unchanged
NEGATIVE: a secret-like word with no assigned value is left alone
NEGATIVE: an email address is not mistaken for userinfo
an over-long line is truncated to 200 characters
a backtick-quoted bearer token in a template literal is masked, not leaked
an api_key value embedded in a backtick template literal is masked
an apikey value wrapped in backticks is masked like the quoted forms
a Bearer-prefixed value joined by a hyphen, with no space delimiter, is still masked
a Bearer-prefixed value joined by a dot, with no space delimiter, is still masked
a quoted Basic credential is masked and the Basic keyword survives
line: const r = await fetch('https://api.acme.com/v1/x', { headers: { Authorization: 'Basic ZGV2OnMzY3JldFAhc3M=' } });
expect: const r = await fetch('https://api.acme.com/v1/x', { headers: { Authorization: 'Basic ••••' } });

label: a backtick-quoted Basic credential is masked the same way
a double-quoted Basic credential is masked the same way
a quoted Token scheme credential is masked and the Token keyword survives
a backtick-quoted Token scheme credential is masked the same way
a quoted Digest credential is masked and the Digest keyword survives
a backtick-quoted Digest credential is masked the same way
a quoted ApiKey scheme credential is masked and the ApiKey keyword survives
a backtick-quoted ApiKey scheme credential is masked the same way
a quoted SSWS credential is masked and the SSWS keyword survives
a backtick-quoted SSWS credential is masked the same way
a quoted AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 credential is masked and the scheme keyword survives
a backtick-quoted AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 credential is masked the same way
a camelCase privateKey value is masked
line: const c = new Client({ endpoint: 'https://api.acme.com', privateKey: 'MIIEvQIBADANBgkq' });
expect: const c = new Client({ endpoint: 'https://api.acme.com', privateKey: '••••' });

label: a snake_case private_key value is masked
a backtick-quoted private_key value is masked
a credential value is masked
a plural credentials value is masked
a pwd value is masked
a Slack webhook path is masked down to its routing prefix
a backtick-quoted Slack webhook path is masked the same way
a Discord webhook id and token are masked
a Zapier catch-hook path is masked
a Teams inbound webhook path is masked
NEGATIVE: a non-webhook path on a webhook host keeps its segments
NEGATIVE: a scheme keyword opening an ordinary sentence keeps its next word
line: // Basic auth is disabled; Digest handshakes are rejected outright
expect: // Basic auth is disabled; Digest handshakes are rejected outright
